# The Lockfile and Cache

The `Cargo.lock` file, the Package ID Spec format, the `~/.cargo` cache layout, and reproducible build flags.

> **Source of truth**: <https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/workflows.html> and the cargo source (`cargo/core/resolver/encode.rs`).

## Cargo.lock

`Cargo.lock` records the **exact versions** of all dependencies in a build. Cargo generates and maintains it — you should not edit it manually. It ensures reproducible builds by pinning dependencies.

### TOML Structure

```toml
# This file is automatically @generated by Cargo.
# It is not intended for manual editing.
version = 4

[[package]]
name = "bitflags"
version = "1.4.3"
source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
checksum = "dcee6df3c840e5d4f92fe6a24b5f74f2c50cc8b2e979c6ec92b5dd5c4d65ed8"
dependencies = [
 "cfg-if",
]

[[package]]
name = "my-crate"
version = "0.1.0"
dependencies = [
 "bitflags",
]
```

### Top-Level Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `version` | int | Lockfile format version (1, 2, 3, or 4). V4 is current default (since Rust 1.83). |
| `[[package]]` | array of tables | One entry per package in the resolved graph |
| `[metadata]` | table | Checksum storage in V1 format (inlined in `[[package]]` for V2+) |
| `[patch]` | table | Tracks which `[patch]` directives from `Cargo.toml` were applied |

### `[[package]]` Entry

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Package name |
| `version` | string | Exact SemVer version |
| `source` | string (optional) | Source identifier — `null`/absent for path/workspace deps |
| `checksum` | string (optional) | SHA-256 of the `.crate` archive (registry deps) |
| `dependencies` | array | List of dependency references (names or inline tables) |
| `replace` | table (optional) | Replace directive |

### Source Identifier Formats

| Source | Format |
|--------|--------|
| Registry (git index) | `registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index` |
| Registry (sparse) | `sparse+https://index.crates.io/` |
| Git | `git+https://github.com/user/repo?branch=dev#abc123` |
| Path / workspace member | (absent — no `source` field) |

### Dependency References

**V2+ (compact, default):**
```toml
dependencies = [
 "cfg-if",
 "log 0.4.20",
]
```

**V1 (inline tables with source):**
```toml
dependencies = [
 { name = "libc", version = "0.2.0", source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index" },
]
```

## Lockfile Format Versions

| Version | Key change |
|---------|------------|
| **V1** | Original. Checksums in `[metadata]` table; full dependency arrays with version info |
| **V2** | Merge-friendly (PR [#7070](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/pull/7070)). Compact dependencies (just names); inline checksums; reduces merge conflicts |
| **V3** | Git branch encoding. `branch = 'master'` in manifest encoded as `?branch=master` in source URLs. Default from Rust 1.53 through 1.82 |
| **V4** | URL encoding fix (PR [#12852](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/pull/12852)). SourceId URL serialization aware of URL encoding. **Current default** (since Rust 1.83) |

Cargo reads all older formats and only upgrades the version when the default changes. Newer Cargo won't unnecessarily rewrite an older lockfile.

## Should You Commit Cargo.lock?

| Project type | Commit? | Why |
|--------------|---------|-----|
| Binary (application) | **Yes** | Ensures reproducible builds |
| Library | Convention says no, but increasingly yes | Downstream generates their own; committing is not harmful and many libraries now commit it |

## Reproducible Build Flags

| Flag | Lockfile must exist? | Must be unchanged? | Network allowed? |
|------|---------------------|-------------------|------------------|
| *(none)* | Optional | No (will update) | Yes |
| `--locked` | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| `--offline` | Optional | No (will update if possible) | No |
| `--frozen` | Yes | Yes | No |

`--frozen` is equivalent to `--locked` + `--offline`.

## Lockfile Management Commands

### `cargo generate-lockfile`

Generate a new `Cargo.lock` or update the existing one to the latest compatible versions. Does not build anything.

### `cargo update`

Update dependencies in `Cargo.lock`:

```sh
cargo update                    # update all deps to latest compatible
cargo update -p serde           # update only serde
cargo update -p serde --precise 1.0.85   # pin to exact version
cargo update --dry-run          # preview without writing
cargo update -w                 # only update workspace packages
cargo update -b serde           # allow SemVer-breaking update (unstable)
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-p, --package <SPEC>` | Package to update (repeatable) |
| `--precise <VERSION>` | Pin to exact version |
| `--recursive` / `--aggressive` | Also update all deps of the spec |
| `-b, --breaking` | Allow SemVer-breaking updates (unstable) |
| `-w, --workspace` | Only update workspace packages |
| `-n, --dry-run` | Don't write the lockfile |

## Package ID Spec

A string that uniquely identifies a package within a dependency graph. Used by `-p` flags, `cargo pkgid`, and `cargo metadata`.

### Short-Form Patterns

| Pattern | Example | Matches |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `name` | `serde` | Any version of `serde` (must be unique in graph) |
| `name@version` | `serde@1.0` | Version requirement |
| `url#version` | `https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo#0.33.0` | URL + version |
| `url#name` | `https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index#bitflags` | URL + name |
| `url#name@version` | `...#crates-io@0.21.0` | URL + name + version |

### Fully Qualified Examples (from `cargo metadata`)

| Source | Example |
|--------|---------|
| Path dependency | `file:///path/to/my-package#0.1.0` |
| Registry | `registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index#regex@1.4.3` |
| Git | `git+https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo?rev=abc123#abc123` |

### Grammar

```
spec    := name [ ("@" | ":") version ] | url [ "#" (name | version | name "@" version) ]
url     := [ kind "+" ] proto "://" host-and-path [ "?" query ]
query   := ("branch" | "tag" | "rev") "=" ref
kind    := "registry" | "git" | "path"
```

`cargo pkgid -p <spec>` prints the fully qualified form. See [commands.md](commands.md).

## The Cargo Home (`~/.cargo`)

Configurable via `CARGO_HOME` (default `~/.cargo`).

### `registry/`

| Path | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `registry/index/` | Cloned registry index (git repo for git protocol, or sparse HTTP cache) |
| `registry/cache/` | Downloaded `.crate` archives (gzip-compressed) |
| `registry/src/` | Extracted source code (unpacked from `.crate`) |

### `git/`

| Path | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `git/db/` | Bare git repos for git dependencies |
| `git/checkouts/` | Checked-out commits (one per rev); multiple checkouts of the same repo coexist |

### Other

| Path | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `bin/` | Executables installed via `cargo install` |
| `config.toml` | Global config — see [config.md](config.md) |
| `credentials.toml` | Auth tokens — see [registries.md](registries.md) |
| `.crates.toml` / `.crates2.json` | Tracking info for `cargo install` |
| `target/` | (per-project, not in `CARGO_HOME`) Build artifacts |

### CI Caching

Cache: `.crates.toml`, `.crates2.json`, `bin/`, `registry/index/`, `registry/cache/`, `git/db/`.
Do **not** cache: `registry/src/` (redundant with `registry/cache/`).

## Cache Auto-Cleaning

Cargo automatically cleans the global cache (configurable via `cache.auto-clean-frequency`):

- Network files (registry `.crate` archives, git checkouts) unused for **3 months** are deleted
- Non-network files unused for **1 month** are deleted
- Disabled when `--offline`

## The Target Directory

Build artifacts go to `target/` (configurable via `build.target-dir` or `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`):

| Path | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `target/debug/` | `dev` profile artifacts |
| `target/release/` | `release` profile artifacts |
| `target/<custom-profile>/` | Custom profile artifacts |
| `target/debug/deps/` | Compiled dependencies and test binaries |
| `target/doc/` | Generated documentation |
| `target/package/` | `.crate` tarballs from `cargo package` |
| `target/<profile>/.fingerprint/` | Fingerprint data for incremental builds |
| `target/<profile>/build/` | Build script output and artifacts |

See [profiles.md](profiles.md) for the profile-to-directory mapping.
